YTBAE002's `PROC_RECONCIL_MESS_ADD` + `GT_RECONCIL_MESS` is the reference implementation. 5) Test every new recon / BDC-chained program from a field-office VPN before release; HQ LAN testing is necessary but not sufficient.

rule: When D01 ADT HTTP Basic authentication returns 401 (password locked, expired, or account disabled), do NOT retry the ADT endpoint. Fall back to P01 SNC/SSO + pyrfc + `RPY_PROGRAM_READ` / `RPY_CLASS_READ` / `RPY_FUNCTIONMODULE_READ`. The same source code is visible in both systems because D01 and P01 remain in sync via transport; reading from P01 yields identical bytes for any object that has been promoted. The auth channel differs (SNC/Kerberos vs HTTP Basic), not the content.
why: Session #057 (2026-04-20): JP_LOPEZ's D01 HTTP Basic password was locked during the INC-000006906 source-extraction step. Two options existed: (a) unlock the password and retry ADT, (b) switch to P01 SNC/SSO which was already working. Option (b) took ~3 minutes (import pyrfc, open connection, call RPY_PROGRAM_READ) and produced the same 3,422-line YTBAE002.abap source. Option (a) would have required a password-reset round-trip (~day). The pattern is re-usable: any time D01 ADT 401s, P01 RFC is a same-day alternative for any object that P01 has (which is true for anything released through the D01→TS1→TS3→P01 chain).
how_to_apply: 1) On any D01 ADT HTTP 401 or 'password locked' response, do not retry ADT; switch to pyrfc + P01 SNC/SSO + RPY_PROGRAM_READ and continue. 2) The connection pattern is already documented in `Zagentexecution/sap_data_extraction/rfc_helpers.py`. 3) Write the extracted source to `extracted_code/...` normally; add a manifest note that source_system='P01' so the next agent knows. 4) Only retry D01 ADT if the object in question exists ONLY on D01 (e.g., in-flight customizing not yet transported). 5) Do not spend cycles troubleshooting D01 auth when a same-content alternative exists on P01.

rule: TIME_OUT dumps in SAP-standard LDB callback forms (PUT_BSIS / PUT_BSAS / PUT_BSAD / PUT_BSIK / PUT_BSAK / PUT_BKPF / PUT_COEP / similar) frequently misdirect the analyst. The work-process IS waiting in that SELECT at the moment the dispatcher kills it, but the clock may have been consumed elsewhere: the CALLER's GET-handler doing a CALL TRANSACTION (MODE 'E' handshake over WAN — see `feedback_mode_e_bdc_is_network_coupling_risk`), a COMMIT WORK AND WAIT blocking on replication, an RFC roundtrip to a slow destination, or a synchronous HTTP call from a BAdI. Before filing an 'LDB performance tuning' ticket, inspect what the caller's handler (the GET event, the LDB_PROCESS callback, the NODES include) is doing for each row delivered.
why: Session #058 (2026-04-20): INC-000006906 dump stack bottomed out at SAPDBSDF:1983 PUT_BSIS. For multiple sessions this was read as 'LDB is slow on BSIS / YBANK set is miscoded / empty-range triggers full scan'. All three were wrong. The LDB was entirely fine; the caller (YTBAE002's GET bsis handler) was synchronously opening SAPGUI dialogs on BDC errors via MODE 'E' CALL TRANSACTIONs. The stack frame was an honest pointer to 'where the WP was waiting' but NOT to 'what consumed the time'.
how_to_apply: 1) On any TIME_OUT whose deepest non-standard frame is still SAP-owned (include name starts with SAPDB, SAPL, SAPM, or RFBIBL / RSDB / etc.), pivot IMMEDIATELY to reading the CALLER's code: what program is driving the LDB? What does its GET-handler / callback form do for each row? 2) Specifically check for: `CALL TRANSACTION` (MODE 'E' is worst), `COMMIT WORK AND WAIT`, `CALL FUNCTION ... DESTINATION <remote>`, `CALL TRANSFORMATION`, synchronous BAdI dispatches with `cl_bad_*`. Each of these is a per-row cost multiplier. 3) Compute an order-of-magnitude estimate: if 1 row delivery triggers a 500 ms network round-trip and the LDB delivers 1,000 rows, the WP will exceed a 5-minute cap cleanly. 4) Only after the caller's handler is cleared should LDB tuning (indexes, WHERE pushdown, SELECT * vs INTO TABLE) be considered.

rule: Every substantive agent<->user turn must be captured as a first-class interaction record in brain_v2/interactions/interactions.json with full preserved_full_text. Session-level retros are derived from this layer; they do NOT replace it.
why: Session #055 almost lost the VRSD->E071 pivot knowledge; it survived only because the user asked. Session #058 captured INC-000006906 root cause via turn-level reasoning but only because the agent manually wrote each hypothesis. Violation mode: when a session ends abruptly, tentative hypotheses/corrections/user pushback evaporates — irreversible loss per CP-001. User explicitly directed Session #059: 'we can not compress or lose knowledge we need to efficiently relate every interaction and create more knowledge'.
how_to_apply: Turn types to capture: hypothesis_test, correction, claim_draft, question_asked, question_answered, tool_trace, decision, user_pushback, user_knowledge_contribution, knowledge_preservation_directive. For each: assign INT-<sess>-<turn> ID, 3-axis domain, preserved_full_text (literal quote, NO summary), evidence list (typed refs), links to claims/rules/KUs/incidents/objects. Append to interactions.json immediately — do not batch at session close. Session close flushes _per_session_index, _per_domain_index, _per_object_index reverse indexes.

rule: Every brain node (object, rule, claim, incident, known_unknown, data_quality, superseded, blind_spot, interaction, skill) must carry a non-null domain field using the 3-axis taxonomy {functional, module, process}. At least one axis must be populated. scope='global' is allowed only for truly cross-cutting rules (e.g., core_principles).
why: User directive Session #059: 'Every topic can be assigned to a domain and improve the knowledge or use the knowledge'. Today only objects are domain-tagged (348 occurrences). Rules/claims/KUs/DQs/superseded/skills are untagged — making domain-scoped queries impossible. This is the reason knowledge routing feels ad-hoc: we know WHAT we learned but not WHERE it belongs.
how_to_apply: 3 axes: functional (Treasury/FI/HCM/BCM/Payment/...), module (FI/HCM/TV/MM/BASIS/CTS/...), process (B2R/H2R/P2P/T2R/P2D). Backfill existing 90 rules, 54 claims, 7 incidents, 44 skills. On new node creation, refuse to persist without domain. brain_v2/domains/domains.json is the registry and carries reverse indexes per domain.

rule: At session start, after reading brain_state.json, compute domain activation from the user's first substantive prompt using domains.json session_activation_hints. Auto-load knowledge_docs + skills + open KUs/DQs + companions for the activated domain(s). Log the activation decision as an interaction (type=decision).
why: Without this, the agent rediscovers which skills/docs/KUs are relevant on every session — wastes cache tokens, misses links, and causes the 'context loaded but not used' failure mode (the problem Layer 11 incidents solved at its own granularity, now solved at domain granularity). User directive Session #059 anchors domains as the routing substrate.
how_to_apply: 1. Scan user first prompt for keywords in domains.json._session_activation_hints regex map. 2. Produce ordered list of activated domains. 3. For each: read its knowledge_docs, note its skills, enumerate open KUs/DQs, reference its companions. 4. Announce 'Active domain(s)' in text before first tool call. 5. Persist the activation as INT-<sess>-000 interaction. 6. If no match: log as blind_spot candidate 'unclassified_session_scope'.

rule: When migrating an XML payment format to ISO 20022 structured address, default to Hybrid (TwnNm+Ctry mandatory + AdrLine fallback) unless each specific bank spec demands fully-structured. Never assume all banks have identical strictness.
why: CBPR+ Nov 2026 mandate permits Hybrid (structured + AdrLine coexist) as a valid compliance state. The handoff doc for UNESCO session #62 explicitly confirms TwnNm+Ctry are the only hard-required fields. Defaulting to Hybrid preserves rollback safety and accommodates vendor master DQ gaps (UNESCO 5% missing STREET, 68% missing HOUSE_NUM1 but 99.995% have CITY1+COUNTRY). Going to fully-structured by default wastes effort on vendor master cleanup that may not be required by all banks.
how_to_apply: Before committing to 'only structured' scope, (1) collect each bank's written spec for structured-address strictness, (2) measure vendor master DQ per field to know the cost of fully-structured, (3) default to Hybrid per bank unless bank spec is explicit. Re-evaluate stricter modes post-go-live on a per-bank basis.

rule: Structured-address migration in SAP DMEE has THREE distinct technical patterns by XML party, not one. Do not confuse them: (1) Cdtr address is SAP-auto-populated via FPAYHX Z-fields bound to vendor master LFA1+ADRC — DMEE tree config only, no ABAP needed. (2) Dbtr address is NOT in FPAYHX/FPAYH — requires ABAP user exit registered in OBPM4 Event 05 that reads T001→ADRC and writes FPAYHX-ZREF01..ZREF05 (NOT FPAYH-REF). (3) UltmtCdtr (alternative payee) may require a data source decision (vendor master / Z-table / separate user exit) per bank/format.
why: UNESCO handoff doc §5 conflated the three patterns causing scope confusion in session #62. Dbtr requires a new FM while Cdtr does not. Mistaking the two leads to either unnecessary ABAP or missing ABAP — both are schedule-breakers for a Nov 2026 deadline.
how_to_apply: When scoping a structured-address change, decompose per XML party (Dbtr/Cdtr/UltmtCdtr/CdtrAgt) and apply the correct pattern to each. Cdtr = tree-only. Dbtr = tree + user exit + OBPM4 registration. UltmtCdtr = decision-first, spec-second, code-third.

rule: A user exit FM for DMEE can be registered in multiple PMW formats via OBPM4 simultaneously. Write once, reuse across formats. Avoid per-format FM duplication.
why: Handoff doc recommends this; UNESCO has 3 target DMEE trees (SEPA_CT_UNES, CITI DC_V3_01, CGI_XML_CT_UNESCO) that all need the same Dbtr address enrichment logic. Building 3 FMs triples maintenance and drift risk.
how_to_apply: When creating Z_DMEE_UNESCO_DEBTOR_ADDR or similar FMs, register it in OBPM4 for all needed PMW formats using Event 05. Centralize logic; format-specific tweaks go behind IF statements keyed on format name, not in separate FMs.

rule: When designing a new ISO 20022 / pain.001 DMEE tree (or version) that needs structured address, source the structured nodes from FPAYHX-REF01/REF02/REF06 using SAP-std byte offsets — do NOT design a custom UNESCO BAdI for address. The SAP-std GENERIC class (CL_IDFI_CGI_CALL05_GENERIC) populates these REF buffers from ADRC at every Event 05 call: REF01[0..60]=street, [60..80]=building, [80..90]=post_code1, [90..100]=region, [100..110]=house_num1; REF02 has Cdtr equivalents; REF06[0..40]=city. Country classes (FR/DE/IT/GB) only ADD REF14 country-specific data, never override the address layout.
why: Phase 0/1 expert re-evaluation discovered that SEPA structured address was thought to require new ABAP, but the SAP-std generic class already does the address-to-REF mapping universally. Avoiding custom address BAdI keeps the change surgical (config-only for SEPA) and aligned with SAP-supported pattern.
how_to_apply: (1) For a new pain.001 tree: register a payment-medium FM (typically FI_PAYMEDIUM_DMEE_CGI_05) for Event 05 in TFPM042FB. (2) Add DMEE nodes for StrtNm/BldgNb/PstCd/TwnNm/Ctry sourced from FPAYHX-REF01 with the byte-offset layout above (use MP_SC_FLD=REF01 + MP_OFFSET + appropriate length). (3) Verify in DMEE Tx Test mode with a sample FPAYHX payload before deploying to D01.

rule: When classifying any object as ACTIVE or DEAD, the verdict MUST be anchored on P01 runtime evidence — NEVER on D01 existence alone. D01 may carry stale/experimental code that is not active in production. Active = exists in P01 + has a runtime hook (DMEE_TREE_HEAD active version, TFPM042FB Event 05 registration, MP_EXIT_FUNC in DMEE_TREE_NODE, ENHO impl active for BAdI dispatch, T042Z payment method routing).
why: Drift between D01 and P01 is normal in legacy SAP shops. Trust D01 only as a deployment target, not as truth. Multiple times during Phase 0 we found objects in one but not the other — the right question is always: does P01 use it today?
how_to_apply: Before declaring an object active or dead: (1) check P01 DMEE_TREE_HEAD for trees, (2) check TFPM042FB for FM Event 05 registrations, (3) check DMEE_TREE_NODE.MP_EXIT_FUNC for node-level FM hooks, (4) check ENHHEADER/SXS_ATTR for ENHO impl active flag, (5) check T042Z for routing. If any layer confirms P01 use, mark ACTIVE. If no P01 layer references it, mark DEAD or NEEDS_VERIFY (do NOT mark ACTIVE based on D01 presence).
examples: Z_DMEE_EXIT_TAX_NUMBER initially flagged D01-only (no source FT include in P01) -> wrong verdict. Re-check via MP_EXIT_FUNC showed FM IS active in P01 CITI tree. The ====FT include is just D01 leftover; the FM proper exists in P01 via different include.

YCL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_DE class is P01-only; we mark it ACTIVE because Y_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES_DE ENHO + the class source from P01 prove dispatch. D01 absence is what triggers the retrofit transport need.
